items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: {PlayerName}, we have Cartethyia's inspection report here.

options.text: Have you figured out what happened to her?

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Unfortunately, not yet.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Since this case is unprecedented, we'll need more time to fully understand it. But we've confirmed a few things.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: After merging the power of the Sentinel and the Threnodian, she subconsciously absorbs nearby energy whenever she uses her Forte.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Last time, she fought in a Sonoro Sphere tainted by the <te href=851023>Dark Tide</te>. This may explain why she felt the Threnodian's pull growing stronger.

options.text: Perhaps this is exactly what the Fractsidus was after.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm alright. Please don't worry about me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No challenge is too great when your will is strong!

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Trust in science for a simpler solution, Miss Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: For now, she should avoid using her Forte.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Based on the data you sent back, the Inverted Tower may be the ideal place for her recovery.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Yes. That Sonoro Sphere's formation is connected to Imperator, which should help stabilize Cartethyia's energy.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Also, during her examination earlier, Cartethyia sensed traces of the Leviathan's residual energy again.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes. It felt the same as the power I sensed when fighting Mya.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I couldn't pinpoint the source, but it seemed to come from somewhere south of the Black Shores, deep underwater.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: We're narrowing down the search. Once we have a location, we'll inform you immediately.

options.text: I'll set out now. Just send me the rough coordinates.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: …Understood. I'll notify our contact to arrange transport for you.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Please be very careful.

items.speaker: Agon Broadcast
items.text: By the end of this match, the Scratchy will have extended their winning streak to 21. Despite the name, this team of young outlanders has made it this far without a single scratch.

items.speaker: Agon Broadcast
items.text: And their next challenge? Vulcan Mining, one of Septimont's most established teams. Will victory smile on the Scratchy once again? Stay tuned…

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hm? Who's calling?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's from the Black Shores... Let's find a quieter spot.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: These coordinates bring us closer to Ragunna.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Should we hit up our friends there for a hand? They might know a thing or two about this.

options.text: It's better we handle this ourselves.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The more people involved, the more attention we might draw from the Order. Their eyes are everywhere, including on our friends.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Besides, we've seen before how the Rinascitans are more vulnerable to the Threnodian's power. If they get involved, it could put them in danger.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Right... that makes sense.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ahhh... it's all so quiet. Still no sign of the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Well, just holler if you need me.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Greetings, Righteous One. If unanswered questions have left you wary, allow me to guide the path before you.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: With the Divinity's blessing, I follow Their words, for I am bound to Their will.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Blasphemy! I see now how the faithless have led you astray, coaxing you to abandon the righteous path.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm not here to play games. Cut to the chase, what are you after?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Rinascita must be unified and purified, for only then can its people be free from fear and division.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: As Their grace and divine will shine upon us once more, so too has Their divine power returned from the faithless lands of Septimont.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: With this power, I will unite the divided minds and build a paradise where tears no longer fall.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Join me in this great cause, Righteous One. This is also a chance to rid yourself of a foe that has long plagued you.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: The Fractsidus.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Ah, so you and the Fractsidus can't agree on how to split the Threnodian's residual energy.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I serve Their will. It is the only true path.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: As for the hubristic faithless, they deserve no salvation... nor will they obstruct my course...

options.text: I can smell it—whose blood have you spilled?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Despite our shared enemy, you still choose conflict over cooperation.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Ha, I trust what I see. There is no need for me to ally with you.
